title: No-geometry place registry entry
description: Declares a place (currently only the country level) that exists as an entity in the place hierarchy but has NO choropleth geometry attached. Consumed by the frontend country-entity router so `/india` and `/india/<indicator-slug>` routes can render line/scalar primitives without ever asking the map layer for a polygon. Per TODO/20260517-iced-bulk-ingest-and-parity-oracle.md Phase 1 step 3 and ADR-0028 (place-first URL cascade). Distinct from `boundary.sources.schema.json`: that file carries provenance for a sibling GeoJSON; this file declares the deliberate absence of one.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Locks the no-geometry registry-entry shape (name, iso3166, no_geometry, plus §12 envelope). Created for the country-entity rollout — first user is `datasets/boundaries/in/country/IN.json`.

name.description: Human-readable place name in citizen-facing English (e.g. "India").

iso3166.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g. "IN"). Alpha-2 matches the convention already used in `state.schema.json`'s top-level `country` field; alpha-3 is intentionally not carried here to keep one canonical form.

no_geometry.description: Must be `true`. The whole point of this schema is to be the explicit declaration that this place has no choropleth geometry; entries with geometry belong in `frontend/src/lib/maplibre/sources.ts` and the `datasets/boundaries/in/geojson/` snapshot, not here.

sources.description: Provenance per CLAUDE.md §12. Hand-authored registry entries use the empty-array convention.
